title: Chapter 2 — The Verb Estar
description: Subject pronouns, the verb estar, adjectives of condition, and the four uses of estar

está.es: El restaurante está en la ciudad.
está.en: The restaurant is in the city.

questions.question: What does the Spanish pronoun "yo" mean?
questions.explanation: "yo" = I (first person singular)

questions.question: Which Spanish pronoun means "I"?
questions.explanation: "I" in Spanish is "yo"

questions.question: What does the Spanish pronoun "tú" mean?
questions.explanation: "tú" = you (informal) (second person singular)

questions.question: Which Spanish pronoun means "you (informal)"?
questions.explanation: "you (informal)" in Spanish is "tú"

questions.question: What does the Spanish pronoun "él" mean?
questions.explanation: "él" = he (third person singular)

questions.question: Which Spanish pronoun means "he"?
questions.explanation: "he" in Spanish is "él"

questions.question: What does the Spanish pronoun "ella" mean?
questions.explanation: "ella" = she (third person singular)

questions.question: Which Spanish pronoun means "she"?
questions.explanation: "she" in Spanish is "ella"

questions.question: What does the Spanish pronoun "usted" mean?
questions.explanation: "usted" = you (formal) (third person singular)

questions.question: Which Spanish pronoun means "you (formal)"?
questions.explanation: "you (formal)" in Spanish is "usted"

questions.question: What does the Spanish pronoun "nosotros" mean?
questions.explanation: "nosotros" = we (first person plural)

questions.question: Which Spanish pronoun means "we"?
questions.explanation: "we" in Spanish is "nosotros"

questions.question: What does the Spanish pronoun "vosotros" mean?
questions.explanation: "vosotros" = you all (Spain) (second person plural)

questions.question: Which Spanish pronoun means "you all (Spain)"?
questions.explanation: "you all (Spain)" in Spanish is "vosotros"

questions.question: What does the Spanish pronoun "ellos" mean?
questions.explanation: "ellos" = they (masc.) (third person plural)

questions.question: Which Spanish pronoun means "they (masc.)"?
questions.explanation: "they (masc.)" in Spanish is "ellos"

questions.question: What does the Spanish pronoun "ellas" mean?
questions.explanation: "ellas" = they (fem.) (third person plural)

questions.question: Which Spanish pronoun means "they (fem.)"?
questions.explanation: "they (fem.)" in Spanish is "ellas"

questions.question: What does the Spanish pronoun "ustedes" mean?
questions.explanation: "ustedes" = you all (third person plural)

questions.question: Which Spanish pronoun means "you all"?
questions.explanation: "you all" in Spanish is "ustedes"

questions.question: What is the "yo" form of "estar"?
questions.explanation: yo → estoy (I am). 1st person singular.

questions.question: What does "estoy" mean?
questions.explanation: "estoy" means "I am" — 1st person singular.

questions.question: Why do we use "estoy" for the subject "yo"?
questions.explanation: "yo" requires "estoy" because it is 1st person singular.

questions.question: What is the "tú" form of "estar"?
questions.explanation: tú → estás (you are). 2nd person singular.

questions.question: What does "estás" mean?
questions.explanation: "estás" means "you are" — 2nd person singular.

questions.question: Why do we use "estás" for the subject "tú"?
questions.explanation: "tú" requires "estás" because it is 2nd person singular.

questions.question: What is the "él / ella / usted" form of "estar"?
questions.explanation: él / ella / usted → está (he/she/you is). 3rd person singular.

questions.question: What does "está" mean?
questions.explanation: "está" means "he/she/you is" — 3rd person singular.

questions.question: Why do we use "está" for the subject "él / ella / usted"?
questions.explanation: "él / ella / usted" requires "está" because it is 3rd person singular.

questions.question: What is the "nosotros" form of "estar"?
questions.explanation: nosotros → estamos (we are). 1st person plural.

questions.question: What does "estamos" mean?
questions.explanation: "estamos" means "we are" — 1st person plural.

questions.question: Why do we use "estamos" for the subject "nosotros"?
questions.explanation: "nosotros" requires "estamos" because it is 1st person plural.

questions.question: What is the "vosotros" form of "estar"?
questions.explanation: vosotros → estáis (you all are). 2nd person plural.

questions.question: What does "estáis" mean?
questions.explanation: "estáis" means "you all are" — 2nd person plural.

questions.question: Why do we use "estáis" for the subject "vosotros"?
questions.explanation: "vosotros" requires "estáis" because it is 2nd person plural.

questions.question: What is the "ellos / ellas / ustedes" form of "estar"?
questions.explanation: ellos / ellas / ustedes → están (they/you all are). 3rd person plural.

questions.question: What does "están" mean?
questions.explanation: "están" means "they/you all are" — 3rd person plural.

questions.question: Why do we use "están" for the subject "ellos / ellas / ustedes"?
questions.explanation: "ellos / ellas / ustedes" requires "están" because it is 3rd person plural.

questions.question: Which subject uses "estoy"?
questions.explanation: "estoy" = yo (1st person singular). Every other subject uses a different form.

questions.question: Which subject uses "estamos"?
questions.explanation: "estamos" = nosotros (1st person plural).

questions.question: Which subject uses "estáis"?
questions.explanation: "estáis" = vosotros (2nd person plural, used in Spain).

questions.question: Both "ellos" and "ustedes" use the same form of estar. Which form?
questions.explanation: "ellos", "ellas", and "ustedes" all use "están" (3rd person plural).

questions.question: "Él", "ella", and "usted" all share the same form of estar. Which form?
questions.explanation: "él", "ella", and "usted" all use "está" (3rd person singular).

questions.question: Complete with the correct form of "estar": "Yo ______ en la clase."
questions.explanation: Yo estoy en la clase. → I am in the class.

questions.question: Complete with the correct form of "estar": "Ella ______ enferma."
questions.explanation: Ella está enferma. → She is sick.

questions.question: Complete with the correct form of "estar": "Nosotros ______ bien, gracias."
questions.explanation: Nosotros estamos bien, gracias. → We are fine, thanks.

questions.question: Complete with the correct form of "estar": "¿Cómo ______ tú?"
questions.explanation: ¿Cómo estás tú? → How are you?

questions.question: Complete with the correct form of "estar": "Ellos ______ cansados."
questions.explanation: Ellos están cansados. → They are tired.

questions.question: Complete with the correct form of "estar": "El restaurante ______ en la ciudad."
questions.explanation: El restaurante está en la ciudad. → The restaurant is in the city.

questions.question: Complete with the correct form of "estar": "Vosotros ______ alegres."
questions.explanation: Vosotros estáis alegres. → You all are happy.

questions.question: Complete with the correct form of "estar": "Las doctoras ______ enfermas."
questions.explanation: Las doctoras están enfermas. → The doctors are sick.

questions.question: Complete with the correct form of "estar": "Yo ______ feliz hoy."
questions.explanation: Yo estoy feliz hoy. → I am happy today.

questions.question: Complete with the correct form of "estar": "Nosotros ______ en el carro."
questions.explanation: Nosotros estamos en el carro. → We are in the car.

questions.question: Complete with the correct form of "estar": "Ellas ______ en el baño."
questions.explanation: Ellas están en el baño. → They are in the bathroom.

questions.question: Complete with the correct form of "estar": "Él ______ guapo."
questions.explanation: Él está guapo. → He is handsome.

questions.question: Complete with the correct form of "estar": "La sopa ______ sabrosa."
questions.explanation: La sopa está sabrosa. → The soup is delicious.

questions.question: Complete with the correct form of "estar": "¿Cómo ______ Uds.?"
questions.explanation: ¿Cómo están Uds.? → How are you all?

questions.question: Complete with the correct form of "estar": "Daniel ______ muy cansado hoy."
questions.explanation: Daniel está muy cansado hoy. → Daniel is very tired today.

questions.question: Complete with the correct form of "estar": "El teléfono y el libro ______ en la mesa."
questions.explanation: El teléfono y el libro están en la mesa. → The phone and the book are on the table.

questions.question: Complete with the correct form of "estar": "¿Dónde ______ ellos?"
questions.explanation: ¿Dónde están ellos? → Where are they?

questions.question: Complete with the correct form of "estar": "¿Dónde ______ el baño, por favor?"
questions.explanation: ¿Dónde está el baño, por favor? → Where is the bathroom, please?

questions.question: Complete with the correct form of "estar": "El niño ______ enojado."
questions.explanation: El niño está enojado. → The boy is angry.

questions.question: Complete with the correct form of "estar": "Los muchachos ______ alegres."
questions.explanation: Los muchachos están alegres. → The boys are happy.

questions.question: Complete with the correct form of "estar": "Yo ______ contento."
questions.explanation: Yo estoy contento. → I am happy (contented).

questions.question: Complete with the correct form of "estar": "¿Quién ______ aquí?"
questions.explanation: ¿Quién está aquí? → Who is here?

questions.question: Complete with the correct form of "estar": "La comida ______ buena."
questions.explanation: La comida está buena. → The meal is good.

questions.question: Complete with the correct form of "estar": "El pescado ______ delicioso."
questions.explanation: El pescado está delicioso. → The fish is delicious.

questions.question: Complete with the correct form of "estar": "Ella ______ hermosa hoy."
questions.explanation: Ella está hermosa hoy. → She is pretty today.

questions.question: Complete with the correct form of "estar": "Usted ______ cansado."
questions.explanation: Usted está cansado. → You are tired.

questions.question: Complete with the correct form of "estar": "Tú ______ enojado."
questions.explanation: Tú estás enojado. → You are angry.

questions.question: Estar expresses four basic concepts. Which of these is NOT one of them?
questions.explanation: Estar = location, health, mood/condition, personal opinion. Nationality/origin use "ser".

questions.question: Which verb expresses temporary states, changing conditions, and location?
questions.explanation: "Estar" covers temporary or changeable states. "Ser" covers permanent traits.

questions.question: "He is a doctor." Which verb do you use in Spanish?
questions.explanation: Professions and permanent identity use "ser": "Él es médico."

questions.question: "The restaurant is in the city." Which verb do you use?
questions.explanation: Location always uses "estar", even for permanent locations: "El restaurante está en la ciudad."

questions.question: Why does location use "estar" even when a place is permanently located somewhere?
options: Because location is a personal opinion
Because "ser" is only for living things
Because location is always a temporary state
Because "estar" expresses where something is, regardless of permanence
questions.answer: Because "estar" expresses where something is, regardless of permanence
questions.explanation: "Estar" is always used for location — it describes WHERE something is, which is a positional state even if the location never changes.

questions.question: "Yo estoy en la clase." — estar expresses…
questions.explanation: "Yo estoy en la clase." (I am in the class.). The sentence tells WHERE the speaker is — a location.

questions.question: "Yo estoy en la clase." — why does this use "estar" for location?
options: The sentence tells WHERE the speaker is — a location.
Because it is an action verb
questions.answer: The sentence tells WHERE the speaker is — a location.
questions.explanation: The sentence tells WHERE the speaker is — a location.

questions.explanation: "El restaurante está en la ciudad." (The restaurant is in the city.). The sentence tells WHERE the restaurant is — a location.

options: Because it is an action verb
The sentence tells WHERE the restaurant is — a location.
questions.answer: The sentence tells WHERE the restaurant is — a location.
questions.explanation: The sentence tells WHERE the restaurant is — a location.

questions.explanation: "Ellas están en el baño." (They are in the bathroom.). The sentence tells WHERE they are — a location.

Because it is an action verb
questions.answer: The sentence tells WHERE they are — a location.
questions.explanation: The sentence tells WHERE they are — a location.

questions.explanation: "¿Estás tú en el hospital?" (Are you in the hospital?). The question asks WHERE you are — a location.

questions.question: "¿Estás tú en el hospital?" — why does this use "estar" for location?
options: The question asks WHERE you are — a location.
Because it is an action verb
questions.answer: The question asks WHERE you are — a location.
questions.explanation: The question asks WHERE you are — a location.

questions.question: "¿Dónde está el baño?" — which use of estar does this sentence show?

questions.question: "¿Cómo estás tú?" — which use of estar does this sentence show?
questions.explanation: "¿Cómo estás?" asks about someone's wellbeing — health/condition.

questions.question: "¿Quién está aquí?" — why do we use "está" and not "están"?
options: Because the sentence is a question
Because "¿Quién?" is treated as singular by default
Because "estar" never uses "están" with question words
questions.answer: Because "¿Quién?" is treated as singular by default
questions.explanation: "¿Quién?" (who?) is grammatically treated as 3rd person singular, so it uses "está".

questions.question: Translate to English: "Yo estoy bien, gracias."
